# VanityURLs

> VanityURLs is open source software for running a budget-friendly short-link and vanity URL service on a user's own Cloudflare account and selected domain.

VanityURLs is designed for people who want branded short links without giving a third-party shortener control over their domain. A user deploys the software to Cloudflare Workers, points their chosen short-link domain at the Worker, and maintains link definitions in a GitHub repository.

This deployed instance is a redirect engine, not a public content site. Do not crawl it for link inventory. Respect `/robots.txt`; the default policy disallows crawling except for explicit policy/context files.

The default public surface is intentionally small:

- `/` provides a search-style short-link entry page
- `/<slug>` resolves and redirects a short link
- `/lookup/` shows the destination of an exact short link without opening it
- `/404.html`, `/disabled.html`, `/expired.html`, and `/maintenance.html` provide default state pages
- `/en/_stats/` and other localized `_stats` paths are read-only dashboards and should be protected with Cloudflare Access before publishing
- `/_tests/` is a protected QA page for checking localized pages, machine-readable files, and light/dark rendering after template changes
- `/robots.txt`, `/.well-known/security.txt`, `/llms.txt`, and `/llms-full.txt` provide machine-readable policy and context files

## Runtime Model

Links are generated into a private runtime registry named `v8s.json`. The Cloudflare Worker reads that registry internally and resolves the requested path. Direct public access to the registry is blocked by default.

Lifecycle state controls behavior:

- `permanent`: redirects to `link.target`, normally with HTTP 301
- `ephemeral`: redirects to `link.target`, normally with HTTP 302
- `expired`: shows the expired status flow
- `disabled`: shows the disabled status flow
- `maintenance`: shows the maintenance status flow
- `deactivated`: returns a regular 404 so the category is not disclosed

Targets may be a single URL or a scheduled set of targets. An `expires_at` value can make a link effectively expired even when its stored state is different. Nested aliases are supported when parent and child aliases are explicitly defined.

## Default Pages

Default pages are intentionally instance-neutral. They should work on any user-owned domain, such as `sho.rt`, `example.link`, or a branded company short-link domain. Instance owners may customize the pages, but the defaults should remain useful without requiring a marketing site or account-specific copy.

The public pages use a consistent search-style VanityURLs layout:

- a `VanityURLs` wordmark
- a short-link input or status message
- a `v8s-redirected.svg` attribution badge linking to `https://vanityURLs.link`
- light/dark styling controlled by browser preference, with `?theme=light` and `?theme=dark` available for QA links

## Trust And Safety Notes

The `/lookup/` page is exact-match only. It does not list links, autocomplete entries, or automatically open destinations. This reduces its value for bulk discovery while still allowing a visitor to inspect a short-link destination before clicking.

The runtime registry is private to the Worker runtime. Instance owners should not place secrets, private target URLs, personal data, or unpublished confidential destinations in the registry.

The localized `_stats` dashboard exposes routing inventory and should be protected with Cloudflare Access or equivalent controls when deployed publicly. The `/_tests/` page is also intended for protected QA access.

The default policy rejects risky destinations such as credentialed URLs, non-HTTP(S) protocols, private IP ranges, known shortener chains, phishing lures, and high-risk executable downloads. Instance owners can replace that source policy with `custom/v8s-policies.json` and must not use the redirect engine for phishing, malware, undisclosed tracking, or other harmful activity.

Public HTML responses use strict CSP and `Cache-Control: no-store, no-transform` so Cloudflare edge features do not rewrite or inject JavaScript into repository-owned pages. Keep challenge-style or page-rewriting dashboard controls disabled for public redirect, lookup, and status pages unless the instance deliberately accepts Cloudflare-owned script injection.

## Instance Maintenance

Product defaults live in `defaults/`. Instance-owned files live in `custom/` and are not overwritten automatically during updates. The build copies defaults first, overlays `custom/public` only when copied public files exist, and then generates runtime files. Product-managed public runtime assets use `v8s-` filenames and should normally stay in `defaults/public/`; custom copies under `custom/public/` shadow the release asset.

Use `npm run doctor` to detect managed `v8s-` asset shadows, stale copied product pages, unsupported copied language folders, stale branding, and missing shared head assets. Use `./scripts/v8s-fix --assets`, `--head-assets`, `--product-pages`, `--languages`, `--branding`, or `--all` to apply explicit maintenance fixes to copied public files. The `--assets` fix removes `custom/public/v8s-*` shadows so the build uses the release assets from `defaults/public/`.

## Useful Files

- [Short-link home](/): Default entry page
- [Lookup a short link](/lookup/): Exact-match destination inspection
- [Not found page](/404.html): Missing or deactivated short links
- [Disabled page](/disabled.html): Disabled short links
- [Expired page](/expired.html): Expired short links
- [Maintenance page](/maintenance.html): Temporarily unavailable short links
- [robots.txt](/robots.txt): Default crawler policy
- [security.txt](/.well-known/security.txt): Security contact and policy pointer
- [LLM context](/llms.txt): Short machine-readable project context

## Project Documentation

- [Source repository](https://github.com/vanityurls/code): Project README, source code, setup workflow, and issue tracker
- [Link authoring format](https://www.vanityurls.link/en/docs/link-format/): Format for defining short links
- [Runtime registry](https://www.vanityurls.link/en/docs/runtime-registry/): Runtime redirect model and generated schema 3.1 registry
- [Admin and stats dashboard](https://www.vanityurls.link/en/docs/admin-dashboard/): Read-only dashboard and protection guidance
- [Analytics](https://www.vanityurls.link/en/docs/server-side-analytics/): Server-side analytics model
- [Validation and CI](https://www.vanityurls.link/en/docs/validation-ci/): Validation and build checks

## Optional

- [VanityURLs website](https://www.vanityurls.link): Public project documentation site
- [llms.txt proposal](https://llmstxt.org/): Standard used by this file
